name: scientific-writing
tagline: Guides scientific manuscript writing (IMRAD, citations, reporting guidelines) and mandates AI-generated figures via companion skills.
verdict: Well-structured writing guidance with a concrete two-stage outline-to-prose workflow, worked examples, and content split into reference files. Weak points: it mandates extensive AI image generation via other skills beyond what the description discloses, gives no failure-path guidance for its external skill/script dependencies, and leaves some bulky content.

highlights.summary: Description only mentions 'figures/tables'; body mandates (line 36) generating graphical abstracts plus 5-30 AI images per doc via other skills, an undisclosed material side effect with API-cost impli
No guidance anywhere for failures of dependencies like the research-lookup skill (line 274) or the figure-generation scripts (OPENROUTER_API_KEY missing, script errors).
Field-specific terminology (lines ~503-587) and full LaTeX box/table examples (line 401 onward, duplicating assets/) kept inline despite the skill already using references/ for other bulk content.
